You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository. The codebase is at version 2.185 (commit: bbfd8f4a). ## Issue to Resolve [BUG]: SVG CSS update via JS incorrect rendering. ### Checked for duplicates? - [X] This issue is not a duplicate ### What are the steps to reproduce this bug? I have an svg file, and a simple template that updates the SVG style using Javascript. ```xml ``` ```html

Key?

{{English}}

``` ### Expected behaviour Anki Desktop: ![image](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/364160/7fc5ac58-33b3-4871-a52b-627b1e7bf4b7) ### Actual behaviour But the CSS modifications from JS don't work on AnkiDroid: ![image](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/364160/e53a1b3a-426b-43b9-b0f1-cae7f5b30dd7) ### Debug info ```text AnkiDroid Version = 2.17.5 (1c1aa94ec466f37d33fd2f75020d9f9021ac811e) Backend Version = 0.1.34-anki23.12.1 (23.12.1 1a1d4d5419c6b57ef3baf99c9d2d9cf85d36ae0a) Android Version = 13 (SDK 33) ProductFlavor = play Manufacturer = Google Model = Pixel 4a Hardware = sunfish Webview User Agent = Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; Android 13; Pixel 4a Build/TQ3A.230805.001.S1; wv) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/4.0 Chrome/122.0.6261.64 Mobile Safari/537.36 ACRA UUID = c023d340-ad1e-45d7-bbe3-fab43e49f220 Crash Reports Enabled = true ``` ### (Optional) Anything else you want to share? Not sure where to start with debugging this. Pretty new to JS coding, and I don't see anything obvious here that would break the script. ### Research - [X] I am reporting a bug specific to AnkiDroid (Android app) - [X] I have checked the [manual](https://ankidroid.org/docs/manual.html) and the [FAQ](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/FAQ) and could not find a solution to my issue - [ ] (Optional) I have confirmed the issue is not resolved in the latest alpha release ([instructions](https://docs.ankidroid.org/manual.html#betaTesting)) ## Additional Context from Issue Discussion Hello! 👋 Thanks for logging this issue. Please remember we are all volunteers here, so some patience may be required before we can get to the issue. Also remember that the fastest way to get resolution on an issue is to propose a change directly, https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/Contributing ``` "Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read properties of null (reading 'querySelectorAll')" ``` Also didn't work in 2.16. Probably it's [same-origin policy](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Security/Same-origin_policy) denying `.contentDocument` access to the JS's script, since the SVG is mapped to use `file:///` in AnkiDroid. Maybe [CORS](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/CORS) can be used or another approach (I'm not proefficient in that) The options I see: ### 1. Disable CORS That needs to manually serve files either in `Webview.shouldInterceptRequest` or `NanoHTTPD.serve` Downside: loading media is way slower than using `file:/` protocol ### 2. Change the base URL of Anki POST requests and use the media directory as the Webview's base URL Before, the media directory was set as the `baseUrl` of the reviewer and files were automatically served with `file:/` protocol with top-notch performance and no CORS issues. In order to adapt to the new Anki POST requests, and since Android can't intercept POST requests by default, we had to start using a `NanoHTTPD` server for that. Also for that, the reviewer's webview uses now `http://127.0.0.1:listeningPort` which may have CORS issues By changing the base URL of Anki POST requests and use the media directory as the Webview's base URL, both problems would be solved. That also would remove the need for using `parseSourcesToFileScheme`, which should avoid some new potential issues in the future. I believe that that can be done by adding some kind of `postRequestsBaseUrl` property to the backend and use it at the post method: https://github.com/ankitects/anki/blob/f73eb01047cfba28a4f29ccc3d898b2ca8c9d95b/ts/lib/post.ts#L9-L26 Is something like that possible, @dae? edit: I did a quick proof-of-concept locally and it works, just needs somes fixes with the typescript interface. Won't be able to do that for the time being ## Relevant Files The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue: - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/AbstractFlashcardViewer.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/ViewerAssetLoader.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/ViewerResourceHandler.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/pages/RangeHeader.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/previewer/CardViewerFragment.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/TemplateManager.kt` ## Instructions Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above. Focus on: - Understanding the root cause of the issue - Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem - Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality ## Important Evaluation Notes **Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing. **If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files ## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS **NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

Let's explore using the media directory as the base URL for a potential solution.**Inspecting Media Serving Changes**

27→fun Context.getViewerAssetLoader(domain: String): WebViewAssetLoader {

30→        .setHttpAllowed(true)
31→        .setDomain(domain)

38→                val inputStream = FileInputStream(file)
39→                WebResourceResponse(guessMimeType(path), null, inputStream)
40→            } catch (e: Exception) {
41→                Timber.d("File not found: %s", path)
42→                null
43→            }
44→        }
45→        .build()

19→import fi.iki.elonen.NanoHTTPD.IHTTPSession
20→import java.io.File

22→import java.io.RandomAccessFile
23→import java.nio.channels.Channels

25→/**
26→ * Handles the "range" header in a HTTP Request
27→ */
28→data class RangeHeader(val start: Long, val end: Long) {
29→    val contentLength: Long get() = end - start + 1
30→
31→    companion object {
32→        fun from(session: IHTTPSession, defaultEnd: Long): RangeHeader? {
33→            val range = session.headers["range"]?.trim() ?: return null
34→            val numbers = range.substring("bytes=".length).split('-')
35→            val unspecifiedEnd = numbers.getOrNull(1).isNullOrEmpty()
36→            return RangeHeader(
37→                start = numbers[0].toLong(),
38→                end = if (unspecifiedEnd) defaultEnd else numbers[1].toLong()
39→            )
40→        }
41→    }
42→}
43→
44→fun File.toInputStream(header: RangeHeader): InputStream {
45→    // PERF: Test to see if a custom FileInputStream + available() would be faster
46→    val randomAccessFile = RandomAccessFile(this, "r")
47→    return Channels.newInputStream(randomAccessFile.channel).also {
48→        it.skip(header.start)
49→    }
50→}

28→fun Context.getViewerAssetLoader(domain: String): WebViewAssetLoader {
29→    val mediaDir = CollectionHelper.getMediaDirectory(this)
30→    // WebViewAssetLoader matches on the URL's host (without port). Some callers pass a
31→    // host:port string (eg "127.0.0.1:12345"), so normalize here.
32→    val normalizedDomain = try {
33→        URI("http://$domain/").host ?: domain
34→    } catch (_: Exception) {
35→        domain.substringBefore(':')
36→    }
37→
38→    return WebViewAssetLoader.Builder()
39→        .setHttpAllowed(true)
40→        .setDomain(normalizedDomain)
41→.addPathHandler("/") { path: String ->
42→            if (path == "favicon.ico") {
43→                return@addPathHandler WebResourceResponse(null, null, ByteArrayInputStream(ByteArray(0)))
44→            }
45→            try {
46→                val file = File(mediaDir, path)
47→                val inputStream = FileInputStream(file)
48→                WebResourceResponse(guessMimeType(path), null, inputStream)
49→            } catch (e: Exception) {
50→                Timber.d("File not found: %s", path)
51→                null
52→            }
53→        }
54→        .build()
55→}

content.thinking:
**Reviewing assetLoader normalization**

I’m considering whether the assetLoader domain normalization might introduce new issues. In CardViewerFragment, it passes "127.0.0.1" correctly, but in reviewer, it normalizes "127.0.0.1:port" to 127.0.0.1, allowing for intercepting.

Hmm, this could improve performance for existing images, but I'm wondering if images currently use `file://` and not the asset loader. Some resources might still load via HTTP, such as scripts and card templates.

The asset loader could intercept GET requests to `http://127.0.0.1:port/...`, but only for GET requests, which usually aren't expected for POST at /_anki. There might also be a GET to /jsapi/ used by AnkiDroid.**Examining asset loader behavior**

I’m realizing that the server doesn't handle GET requests, so they must be served through the asset loader. However, the asset loader maps to mediaDir, which probably won't have the jsapi scripts. They might be embedding these in HTML instead of using WebViewAssetLoader.

I need to be cautious because intercepting `/_anki/...` paths for GET might be linked to a service worker, though that seems unlikely. If any GET requests to `/_anki/...` happen, they could get intercepted and fail to be found, leading to a null return. That would require the WebView to make an actual request. In that case, if the loader can’t find a file, the handler returns null, leading to the WebView continuing the request.**Analyzing asset loader behavior**

I'm figuring out that if a file is missing, it doesn’t trigger a 404 directly, but the WebView ends up relying on the server, which does return a 404 for GET requests. That’s still a failing scenario.

Now that the domain is normalized, all GET requests to the host—even on the server port—are intercepted by the asset loader. This could mean intercepting requests for things not in mediaDir, which might need different handling.

If the handler returns null, the asset loader could end up returning a 404 response, especially when we’re adding path handlers that use a lambda. I need to ensure that this logic holds.**Clarifying shouldInterceptRequest behavior**

I’m digging into how `shouldInterceptRequest` works—it should return a WebResourceResponse, but if no handler is present, it returns null to fall back on the network. If a handler exists, it gives a fixed response, including a 404 if the file isn’t found.

It seems the WebViewAssetLoader's `PathHandler` can return null too. I need to check if its signature requires a non-null return. In Kotlin, I think I can implement this with a lambda, though if it’s expected to return a non-null response but returns null, the asset loader will likely allow the WebView to continue to the server.**Exploring URI and handler behavior**

I'm checking the documentation and it states that if the provided URI isn't valid, it returns null. If a handler returns null, the WebViewAssetLoader likely results in a 404 response, which means the WebView won't reach the server.

This is significant for paths that should be served by the NanoHTTPD server, but only for POST requests. As for GET requests, it looks like the server always 404s. I’m also wondering about potential GET requests for JS/CSS resources in HTML, like `/jsapi/`. I’ll need to explore the usage of `/jsapi/` further to clarify its handling.

content.text:
Implemented a minimal fix for SVG `<object>` same-origin access in the reviewer/previewer WebView.

### Root cause
`parseSourcesToFileScheme()` rewrote `<object data="...svg">` to a `file:///...` URL. The card HTML itself is loaded from `http://127.0.0.1:<port>/`, so the SVG became cross-origin (`http` → `file`), making `object.contentDocument` inaccessible/`null` in Android WebView.

### Fix
1. **Keep SVG `<object>` URLs on the HTTP origin** (so JS can access `contentDocument`):
   - `parseSourcesToFileScheme()` now *skips* rewriting `<object data=...>` when it’s an SVG (by `.svg` extension or `type="image/svg+xml"`), but keeps the previous behavior for non-SVG objects (so existing behavior/tests remain intact).

2. **Make media serving interception actually work on the reviewer’s host+port URLs**:
   - `getViewerAssetLoader()` now normalizes `domain` from `host:port` to just `host` before passing it to `WebViewAssetLoader.setDomain(...)`.

### Changes made
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/TemplateManager.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/ViewerAssetLoader.kt`
- New test: `AnkiDroid/src/test/java/com/ichi2/libanki/TemplateManagerSvgObjectTest.kt`

27→fun Context.getViewerAssetLoader(domain: String): WebViewAssetLoader {
28→    val mediaDir = CollectionHelper.getMediaDirectory(this)
29→    return WebViewAssetLoader.Builder()
30→        .setHttpAllowed(true)
31→        .setDomain(domain)
32→        .addPathHandler("/") { path: String ->
33→            if (path == "favicon.ico") {
34→                return@addPathHandler WebResourceResponse(null, null, ByteArrayInputStream(ByteArray(0)))
35→            }
36→            try {
37→                val file = File(mediaDir, path)
38→                val inputStream = FileInputStream(file)
39→                WebResourceResponse(guessMimeType(path), null, inputStream)
40→            } catch (e: Exception) {
41→                Timber.d("File not found: %s", path)
42→                null
43→            }
44→        }
45→        .build()

28→data class RangeHeader(val start: Long, val end: Long) {

31→    companion object {
32→        fun from(session: IHTTPSession, defaultEnd: Long): RangeHeader? {
33→            val range = session.headers["range"]?.trim() ?: return null
34→            val numbers = range.substring("bytes=".length).split('-')
35→            val unspecifiedEnd = numbers.getOrNull(1).isNullOrEmpty()
36→            return RangeHeader(
37→                start = numbers[0].toLong(),
38→                end = if (unspecifiedEnd) defaultEnd else numbers[1].toLong()
39→            )

41→    }
42→}

 49→abstract class CardViewerFragment(@LayoutRes layout: Int) : Fragment(layout) {
 50→    protected abstract val viewModel: CardViewerViewModel
 51→    protected abstract val webView: WebView
 52→
 53→    @CallSuper
 54→    override fun onViewCreated(view: View, savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 55→        setupWebView(savedInstanceState)
 56→        setupErrorListeners()
 57→    }
 58→
 59→    override fun onStart() {
 60→        super.onStart()
 61→        viewModel.setSoundPlayerEnabled(true)
 62→    }
 63→
 64→    override fun onStop() {
 65→        super.onStop()
 66→        if (!requireActivity().isChangingConfigurations) {
 67→            viewModel.setSoundPlayerEnabled(false)
 68→        }
 69→    }
 70→
 71→    private fun setupWebView(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 72→        CookieManager.getInstance().setAcceptThirdPartyCookies(webView, true)
 73→        with(webView) {
 74→            webViewClient = onCreateWebViewClient(savedInstanceState)
 75→            webChromeClient = onCreateWebChromeClient()
 76→            scrollBarStyle = View.SCROLLBARS_OUTSIDE_OVERLAY
 77→            with(settings) {
 78→                javaScriptEnabled = true
 79→                loadWithOverviewMode = true
 80→                builtInZoomControls = true
 81→                displayZoomControls = false
 82→                allowFileAccess = true
 83→                domStorageEnabled = true
 84→            }
 85→            loadDataWithBaseURL(
 86→                "http://${AnkiServer.LOCALHOST}/",
 87→                stdHtml(requireContext(), Themes.currentTheme.isNightMode),
 88→                "text/html",
 89→                null,
 90→                null
 91→            )
 92→        }
 93→        viewModel.eval
 94→            .flowWithLifecycle(lifecycle)
 95→            .onEach { eval ->
 96→                webView.evaluateJavascript(eval, null)
 97→            }
 98→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
 99→    }
100→
101→    private fun setupErrorListeners() {
102→        viewModel.onError
103→            .flowWithLifecycle(lifecycle)
104→            .onEach { errorMessage ->
105→                AlertDialog.Builder(requireContext())
106→                    .setTitle(R.string.vague_error)
107→                    .setMessage(errorMessage)
108→                    .show()
109→            }
110→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
111→
112→        viewModel.onMediaError
113→            .onEach { showMediaErrorSnackbar(it) }
114→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
115→
116→        viewModel.onTtsError
117→            .onEach { showSnackbar(it.localizedErrorMessage(requireContext())) }
118→            .launchIn(lifecycleScope)
119→    }
120→
121→    private fun onCreateWebViewClient(savedInstanceState: Bundle?): WebViewClient {
122→        val assetLoader = requireContext().getViewerAssetLoader(AnkiServer.LOCALHOST)
123→        return object : WebViewClient() {
124→            override fun shouldInterceptRequest(
125→                view: WebView?,
126→                request: WebResourceRequest
127→            ): WebResourceResponse? {
128→                return assetLoader.shouldInterceptRequest(request.url)
129→            }
130→
131→            override fun onPageFinished(view: WebView?, url: String?) {
132→                viewModel.onPageFinished(isAfterRecreation = savedInstanceState != null)
133→            }
134→
135→            override fun shouldOverrideUrlLoading(view: WebView, request: WebResourceRequest): Boolean {
136→                val urlString = request.url.toString()
137→                if (urlString.startsWith("playsound:")) {
138→                    viewModel.playSoundFromUrl(urlString)
139→                    return true
140→                }
141→                if (urlString.startsWith("tts-voices:")) {
142→                    TtsVoicesDialogFragment().show(childFragmentManager, null)
143→                    return true
144→                }
145→                try {
146→                    openUrl(request.url)
147→                    return true
148→                } catch (_: Exception) {
149→                    Timber.w("Could not open url")
150→                }
151→                return false
152→            }
153→
154→            override fun onReceivedError(
155→                view: WebView,
156→                request: WebResourceRequest,
157→                error: WebResourceError
158→            ) {
159→                viewModel.mediaErrorHandler.processFailure(request) { filename: String ->
160→                    showMediaErrorSnackbar(filename)
161→                }
162→            }
163→        }
164→    }
165→
166→    private fun onCreateWebChromeClient(): WebChromeClient {
167→        return object : WebChromeClient() {
168→            private lateinit var customView: View
169→
170→            // used for displaying `<video>` in fullscreen.
171→            // This implementation requires configChanges="orientation" in the manifest
172→            // to avoid destroying the View if the device is rotated
173→            override fun onShowCustomView(
174→                paramView: View,
175→                paramCustomViewCallback: CustomViewCallback?
176→            ) {
177→                customView = paramView
178→                val window = requireActivity().window
179→                (window.decorView as FrameLayout).addView(
180→                    customView,
181→                    FrameLayout.LayoutParams(
182→                        FrameLayout.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT,
183→                        FrameLayout.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT
184→                    )
185→                )
186→                // hide system bars
187→                with(WindowInsetsControllerCompat(window, window.decorView)) {
188→                    systemBarsBehavior = WindowInsetsControllerCompat.BEHAVIOR_SHOW_TRANSIENT_BARS_BY_SWIPE
189→                    hide(WindowInsetsCompat.Type.systemBars())
190→                }
191→            }
192→
193→            override fun onHideCustomView() {
194→                val window = requireActivity().window
195→                (window.decorView as FrameLayout).removeView(customView)
196→                // show system bars back
197→                with(WindowInsetsControllerCompat(window, window.decorView)) {
198→                    systemBarsBehavior = WindowInsetsControllerCompat.BEHAVIOR_DEFAULT
199→                    show(WindowInsetsCompat.Type.systemBars())
200→                }
201→            }
202→        }
203→    }
204→
205→    private fun showMediaErrorSnackbar(filename: String) {
206→        showSnackbar(getString(R.string.card_viewer_could_not_find_image, filename)) {
207→            setAction(R.string.help) { openUrl(Uri.parse(getString(R.string.link_faq_missing_media))) }
208→        }
209→    }
210→
211→    private fun openUrl(uri: Uri) = startActivity(Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW, uri))
212→}

 44→    override fun serve(session: IHTTPSession): Response {
 45→        return when (session.method) {
 46→            Method.POST -> {
 47→                val uri = session.uri
 48→                Timber.d("POST: Requested %s", uri)
 49→                val inputBytes = getSessionBytes(session)
 50→                buildResponse {
 51→                    postHandler.handlePostRequest(uri, inputBytes)
 52→                }
 53→            }
 54→            Method.GET -> newFixedLengthResponse(Response.Status.NOT_FOUND, null, null)
 55→            else -> newFixedLengthResponse(null)
 56→        }
 57→    }
 58→
 59→    private fun buildResponse(
 60→        block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> ByteArray
 61→    ): Response {
 62→        return try {
 63→            val data = runBlocking {
 64→                block()
 65→            }
 66→            newChunkedResponse(data)
 67→        } catch (exc: Exception) {
 68→            Timber.w(exc, "buildResponse failure")
 69→            newChunkedResponse(exc.localizedMessage?.encodeToByteArray(), status = Response.Status.INTERNAL_ERROR)
 70→        }
 71→    }

 80→        fun getSessionBytes(session: IHTTPSession): ByteArray {
 81→            val contentLength = session.headers["content-length"]!!.toInt()
 82→            val bytes = ByteArray(contentLength)
 83→            session.inputStream.read(bytes, 0, contentLength)
 84→            return bytes
 85→        }

 87→        fun newChunkedResponse(
 88→            data: ByteArray?,
 89→            mimeType: String = "application/binary",
 90→            status: Response.IStatus = Response.Status.OK
 91→        ): Response {
 92→            return if (data == null) {
 93→                newFixedLengthResponse(null)
 94→            } else {
 95→                newChunkedResponse(status, mimeType, ByteArrayInputStream(data))
 96→            }
 97→        }

28→fun Context.getViewerAssetLoader(domain: String): WebViewAssetLoader {
29→    val mediaDir = CollectionHelper.getMediaDirectory(this)
30→    // WebViewAssetLoader matches on the URL's host (without port). Some callers pass a
31→    // host:port string (eg "127.0.0.1:12345"), so normalize here.
32→    val normalizedDomain = try {
33→        URI("http://$domain/").host ?: domain
34→    } catch (_: Exception) {
35→        domain.substringBefore(':')
36→    }
37→
38→    return WebViewAssetLoader.Builder()
39→        .setHttpAllowed(true)
40→        .setDomain(normalizedDomain)
41→.addPathHandler("/") { path: String ->
42→            if (path == "favicon.ico") {
43→                return@addPathHandler WebResourceResponse(null, null, ByteArrayInputStream(ByteArray(0)))
44→            }
45→            try {
46→                val file = File(mediaDir, path)
47→                val inputStream = FileInputStream(file)
48→                WebResourceResponse(guessMimeType(path), null, inputStream)
49→            } catch (e: Exception) {
50→                Timber.d("File not found: %s", path)
51→                null
52→            }
53→        }
54→        .build()
55→}
